title: The job I turned down that everyone thought I should take
summary: Narrate and reflect on a moment when you refused a job, course, or opportunity that almost everyone around you expected you to accept.
topic: Turning down a 'good' opportunity that everyone expected you to take
prompt: Write 3 paragraphs (350–500 words) about a job, course, or opportunity you turned down despite almost everyone around you expecting you to accept it. Explain what you saw that they did not. Use the past perfect, second or third conditional, present perfect for reflection, and 'although' or 'however'.
hintStarters: The offer came at the end of a quiet evening in Buenos Aires, and by the next morning almost everyone I told assumed I would take it because…
If I had accepted, the version of my life that would have followed is one I can describe with surprising clarity, and that is partly why…
Although the salary, the title, and the company name all looked exactly like what a person of my age was supposed to want, what I noticed in my own reaction to the offer was…

hintVocab.meaningVi: tự nghi ngờ quyết định của mình
áp lực từ bạn bè / đồng nghiệp
cân nhắc các sự đánh đổi
một dấu hiệu cảnh báo nhẹ
một quyết định bất quy ước
biện minh cho một lựa chọn
tin vào trực giác của bạn
nhìn lại mà không hối tiếc

sampleText:
The offer came at the end of a quiet evening in Buenos Aires, six years ago. A friend's company wanted a senior position filled and someone had recommended me — the salary was double what I was earning, the title was something my parents would have understood the first time, and the city was one I had liked from the moment I arrived. By the next morning, almost everyone I told assumed I would take it. My mother, who had not asked me about money in years, asked me about money. A colleague I trusted said it would be irresponsible to refuse. For three days I tried to feel as enthusiastic as everyone around me clearly was, and the harder I tried, the more clearly I noticed something I had been ignoring all week: a small, persistent feeling that the work itself, behind the salary and the title, was not work I wanted to do for the next five years of my life.

If I had taken the job, I can describe the version of my life that would have followed with surprising clarity. I would have moved into a flat in Palermo, learned the rhythms of Argentine corporate culture, and become competent at a kind of project I had no real curiosity about. The money would have rearranged my parents' relief into pride. I would have stopped feeling that I had to explain myself at family lunches. None of this is imagined; the colleagues I would have had took exactly that path and reported back. What stopped me was that none of them, when I asked carefully, said they were doing work they cared about. They said it was a good job. They said the team was reasonable. They said it was 'where I should be at this stage'. Although these are perfectly honest answers, they are also a particular kind of silence about the thing I was actually asking.

What I have come to understand is that the hardest opportunities to refuse are not the bad ones. They are the ones that look like exactly what a person of your age and background is supposed to want, because refusing them requires you to disagree publicly with a definition of success that the people who love you have already accepted. However, six years on, I am still not sure I made the right decision in some absolute sense. I have less money than I would have had. I have had years that were noticeably harder. What I do have is the quiet certainty that the work I have done since has been work I actually wanted to do, and that turning down the offer was not a refusal of a good life but a refusal of someone else's version of one.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Third conditional for an unrealised past path
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'If I had taken the job, I can describe the version of my life that would have followed' và 'I would have moved into a flat in Palermo' minh hoạ điều kiện loại 3 (had + past participle / would have + past participle) để mô tả một quá khứ không thực sự xảy ra — một kỹ thuật mạnh mẽ trong văn phản tỉnh.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'If I had taken the job, I can describe the version of my life that would have followed' and 'I would have moved into a flat in Palermo' illustrate the third conditional (had + past participle / would have + past participle) for describing an unrealised past — a powerful technique in reflective writing.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Parallel 'the + comparative … the + comparative' structure
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'The harder I tried, the more clearly I noticed something I had been ignoring' dùng cấu trúc song song hai vế comparative để diễn đạt hai sự thay đổi cùng tăng. Đây là kỹ thuật rất hiệu quả để diễn đạt nội tâm phức tạp.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'The harder I tried, the more clearly I noticed something I had been ignoring' uses the parallel 'the + comparative … the + comparative' structure to show two parallel changes. This is a very effective way to express complex internal experience.

sampleTranslationVi:
Lời mời đến vào cuối một buổi tối yên tĩnh ở Buenos Aires, sáu năm trước. Công ty của một người bạn muốn lấp một vị trí cấp cao và ai đó đã giới thiệu tôi — lương gấp đôi cái tôi đang kiếm, chức danh là điều bố mẹ tôi sẽ hiểu ngay lần đầu, và thành phố là một nơi tôi đã thích từ khoảnh khắc tôi đến. Sáng hôm sau, hầu hết mọi người tôi kể đều cho rằng tôi sẽ nhận nó. Mẹ tôi, người đã nhiều năm không hỏi tôi về tiền, đã hỏi tôi về tiền. Một đồng nghiệp tôi tin tưởng nói rằng từ chối sẽ là vô trách nhiệm. Trong ba ngày tôi cố cảm thấy nhiệt tình như mọi người xung quanh rõ ràng đang cảm thấy, và tôi càng cố gắng, tôi càng nhận ra điều mà tôi đã bỏ qua cả tuần: một cảm giác nhỏ và dai dẳng rằng bản thân công việc, đằng sau lương và chức danh, không phải là công việc tôi muốn làm trong năm năm tiếp theo của đời mình.

Nếu tôi đã nhận công việc, tôi có thể miêu tả phiên bản cuộc đời sẽ theo sau với sự rõ ràng đáng ngạc nhiên. Tôi sẽ chuyển vào một căn hộ ở Palermo, học các nhịp điệu của văn hóa doanh nghiệp Argentina, và trở nên thành thạo ở một loại dự án mà tôi không có sự tò mò thực sự. Tiền sẽ sắp xếp lại sự nhẹ nhõm của bố mẹ tôi thành niềm tự hào. Tôi sẽ ngừng cảm thấy phải giải thích bản thân tại các bữa trưa gia đình. Không điều nào trong số này là tưởng tượng; những đồng nghiệp tôi sẽ có đã đi đúng con đường đó và phản hồi lại. Điều ngăn tôi là không ai trong số họ, khi tôi hỏi cẩn thận, nói rằng họ đang làm công việc mình thực sự quan tâm. Họ nói đó là một công việc tốt. Họ nói đội ngũ hợp lý. Họ nói đó là 'nơi tôi nên ở ở giai đoạn này'. Mặc dù đây là những câu trả lời hoàn toàn trung thực, chúng cũng là một kiểu im lặng đặc biệt về điều tôi thực sự đang hỏi.

Điều tôi đã dần hiểu là những cơ hội khó từ chối nhất không phải là những cái xấu. Chúng là những cái trông giống chính xác điều mà một người ở tuổi và xuất thân của bạn được cho là sẽ muốn, bởi vì từ chối chúng đòi hỏi bạn phải bất đồng công khai với một định nghĩa về thành công mà những người yêu thương bạn đã chấp nhận. Tuy nhiên, sáu năm sau, tôi vẫn không chắc tôi đã ra quyết định đúng theo nghĩa tuyệt đối nào đó. Tôi có ít tiền hơn cái tôi sẽ có. Tôi đã trải qua những năm rõ ràng khó khăn hơn. Cái tôi có là sự chắc chắn lặng lẽ rằng công việc tôi đã làm từ đó là công việc tôi thực sự muốn làm, và rằng việc từ chối lời mời không phải là từ chối một cuộc sống tốt mà là một sự từ chối phiên bản của ai khác.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the third conditional correctly?
options: If I took the job, I would have moved into a flat in Palermo.
If I had taken the job, I would have moved into a flat in Palermo.
If I had taken the job, I would moved into a flat in Palermo.
If I had taken the job, I would have move into a flat in Palermo.
mcQuestions.explanation: Third conditional: 'had + past participle' in the if-clause; 'would have + past participle' in the main clause.
mcQuestions.hint: 'If + had + past participle, would have + past participle'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the parallel comparative structure correctly?
options: Harder I tried, the more clearly I noticed something I had been ignoring.
The harder I tried, more clearly I noticed something I had been ignoring.
The harder I tried, the more clearly I noticed something I had been ignoring.
The harder I tried, so the more clearly I noticed something I had been ignoring.
mcQuestions.explanation: The structure is 'the + comparative …, the + comparative …'. Both halves require 'the'; no extra connector.
mcQuestions.hint: Both halves of the parallel comparative must begin with 'the'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the past perfect correctly?
options: I noticed something I had ignoring all week.
I noticed something I have been ignoring all week.
I noticed something I was been ignoring all week.
I noticed something I had been ignoring all week.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect continuous = 'had been + -ing'; describes an ongoing action up to a past reference point.

options: Although these are perfectly honest answers, they are also a particular kind of silence.
Although these are perfectly honest answers, but they are also a particular kind of silence.
Although, these are perfectly honest answers they are also a particular kind of silence.
These are perfectly honest answers, although however they are also a particular kind of silence.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Although + clause, main clause' — no 'but', no comma after 'although', no doubling.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'have come to understand' correctly?
options: What I came to understand is that the hardest opportunities to refuse are not the bad ones.
What I have come to understand is that the hardest opportunities to refuse are not the bad ones.
What I have came to understand is that the hardest opportunities to refuse are not the bad ones.
What I have come understanding is that the hardest opportunities to refuse are not the bad ones.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect 'have come to + base verb'; past participle of 'come' is 'come'.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Have come to + base verb'; past participle of 'come' is 'come'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option uses 'would have understood' correctly?
options: The title was something my parents would understood the first time.
The title was something my parents will have understood the first time.
The title was something my parents would have understood the first time.
The title was something my parents would have understand the first time.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Would have + past participle' is the past hypothetical structure; past participle of 'understand' is 'understood'.

options: I would have stopped to feel that I had to explain myself at family lunches.
I would have stopped to feeling that I had to explain myself at family lunches.
I would have stop feeling that I had to explain myself at family lunches.
I would have stopped feeling that I had to explain myself at family lunches.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Stop + -ing' means to cease an activity. 'Stop + to + infinitive' means to pause in order to do something — a different meaning. After 'would have', use the past participle 'stopped'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'requires + object + to + base verb' correctly?
options: Refusing them requires you to disagree publicly with a definition of success.
Refusing them requires you disagree publicly with a definition of success.
Refusing them requires you that you disagree publicly with a definition of success.
Refusing them requires of you to disagree publicly with a definition of success.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Require + object + to + base verb' is the standard structure.

options: I have less money than I would have.
I have less money than I would have had.
I have fewer money than I would have had.
I have less of money than I would have had.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Less + uncountable noun + than' is the correct collocation; 'money' is uncountable so 'fewer' is wrong; 'would have had' is the past hypothetical perfect.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Less + uncountable noun + than'; 'would have had' for past hypothetical possession.

options: Turning down the offer was no a refusal of a good life but a refusal of someone else's version of one.
Turning down the offer was not a refusal of a good life although a refusal of someone else's version of one.
Turning down the offer was not a refusal of a good life but a refusal of someone else's version of one.
Turning down the offer was not a refusal of a good life however a refusal of someone else's version of one.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Not X but Y' is a fixed contrasting structure. 'No' (instead of 'not') is wrong before a noun phrase; 'although' and 'however' introduce different kinds of clauses.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Not + X + but + Y' for direct contrast.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option correctly uses 'in some absolute sense'?
options: I am still not sure I made the right decision in some absolute the sense.
I am still not sure I made the right decision in some absolutely sense.
I am still not sure I made the right decision at some absolute sense.
I am still not sure I made the right decision in some absolute sense.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'In + adjective + sense' is the correct phrase; 'absolute' (adjective) modifies 'sense' (noun); the preposition is 'in', not 'at'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: If refusing a 'good' opportunity requires you to disagree with a definition of success that the people who love you already accept, is the cost of that disagreement worth it — and how do you tell, in advance, whether it will be?
